heading: Regional conservation partnerships
label: 3871b Regional conservation partnerships

children.heading: Partnership agreements authorized
children.label: a Partnership agreements authorized
children.text: The Secretary may enter into a partnership agreement with an eligible partner to implement a project that will assist producers with installing and maintaining an eligible activity on eligible land.

children.label: A
children.text: for a period not to exceed 5 years; or

children.label: B
children.text: for a period that is longer than 5 years, if the longer period is necessary to meet the objectives of the program, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Renewal
children.label: 2 Renewal
children.text: A partnership agreement may be renewed under subsection (e)(5) for a period not to exceed 5 years.

children.heading: Extension
children.label: 3 Extension
children.text: A partnership agreement, or any renewal of a partnership agreement, may each be extended 1 time for a period not longer than 12 months, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: 1 or more conservation benefits that the project shall achieve;

children.label: ii
children.text: the eligible activities on eligible land to be conducted under the project to achieve conservation benefits;

children.label: iii
children.text: the implementation timeline for carrying out the project, including any interim milestones;

children.label: iv
children.text: the local, State, multistate, or other geographic area covered; and

children.label: v
children.text: the planning, outreach, implementation, and assessment to be conducted;

children.label: B
children.text: conduct outreach and education to producers for potential participation in the project;

children.label: C
children.text: at the request of a producer, act on behalf of a producer participating in the project in applying for assistance under ;section 3871c of this title

children.label: D
children.text: leverage financial or technical assistance provided by the Secretary with additional contributions to help achieve the project objectives;

children.label: i
children.text: the progress made by the project in achieving each conservation benefit defined in the partnership agreement, including in a quantified form to the extent practicable; and

children.label: ii
children.text: as appropriate, other outcomes of the project; and

children.label: F
children.text: at the conclusion of the project, report to the Secretary on its results and funds leveraged.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An eligible partner shall provide a significant portion of the overall costs of the scope of the project that is the subject of the agreement entered into under subsection (a), as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: direct funding;

children.label: ii
children.text: in-kind support; or

children.label: iii
children.text: a combination of direct funding and in-kind support.

children.heading: Treatment
children.label: C Treatment
children.text: Any amounts expended during the period beginning on the date on which the Secretary announces the approval of an application under subsection (e) and ending on the day before the effective date of the partnership agreement by an eligible partner for staff salaries or development of the partnership agreement may be considered to be a part of the contribution of the eligible partner under this paragraph.

children.label: A
children.text: entering into program contracts with producers;

children.label: B
children.text: providing financial assistance to producers; and

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of a partnership agreement that is funded through an alternative funding arrangement or grant agreement under , providing the payments to the eligible partner for carrying out eligible activities;section 3871c(d) of this title
identify in each State a program coordinator for the State, who shall be responsible for providing assistance to eligible partners under the program;
establish guidance to assist eligible partners with carrying out the assessment required under subsection (c)(1)(E);

children.label: A
children.text: a semiannual report describing the status of each pending and obligated contract under the project of the eligible partner; and

children.label: B
children.text: an annual report describing how the Secretary used amounts reserved by the Secretary for that year for technical assistance under section 3871d(f) of this title; and1So in original. Probably should be “section 3871d(e)”.1
ensure that any eligible activity effectively achieves the conservation benefits identified in the partnership agreement under subsection (c)(1)(A)(i).

children.heading: Competitive process
children.label: 1 Competitive process
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct a simplified competitive process to select applications for partnership agreements and may assess and rank applications with similar conservation purposes as a group.

children.heading: Criteria used
children.label: 2 Criteria used
children.text: In carrying out the process described in paragraph (1), the Secretary shall make public the criteria used in evaluating applications.

children.label: A
children.text: the scope of the project, as described in subsection (c)(1)(A);

children.label: B
children.text: the plan for monitoring, evaluating, and reporting on progress made toward achieving the project’s objectives;

children.label: C
children.text: the program resources requested for the project and estimated funding needed from the Secretary;

children.label: D
children.text: each eligible partner collaborating to achieve project objectives, including their roles, responsibilities, capabilities, and contribution; and

children.label: E
children.text: any other elements the Secretary considers necessary to adequately evaluate and competitively select applications for funding under the program.

children.label: A
children.text: assist producers in meeting or avoiding the need for a natural resource regulatory requirement;

children.label: B
children.text: have a high percentage of producers in the area to be covered by the agreement;

children.label: C
children.text: significantly leverage non-Federal financial and technical resources and coordinate with other local, State, or national efforts;

children.label: D
children.text: build new partnerships with local, State, and private entities to include a diversity of stakeholders in the project;

children.label: i
children.text: to achieve conservation benefits; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a project in a critical conservation area under , to address the priority resource concern for that critical conservation area;section 3871f of this title

children.label: F
children.text: implement the project consistent with existing watershed, habitat, or other area restoration plans;

children.label: G
children.text: provide innovation in conservation methods and delivery, including outcome-based performance measures and methods; or

children.label: H
children.text: meet other factors that are important for achieving the purposes of the program, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: to continue to implement the project under a renewal of the partnership agreement; or

children.label: B
children.text: to expand the scope of the project under a renewal of the partnership agreement, as long as the expansion is within the objectives and purposes of the original partnership agreement.

children.heading: Nonapplicability of adjusted gross income limitation
children.label: f Nonapplicability of adjusted gross income limitation
children.text: The adjusted gross income limitation described in shall not apply to an eligible partner under the program.section 1308–3a(b)(1) of title 7
